back_translation: Afterward Moses and Aaron went and said to Pharaoh, 'Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: Let my people go that they may hold a feast to me in the wilderness.'
Pharaoh said, 'Who is the LORD that I should obey his voice and let Israel go? I do not know the LORD, and I will not let Israel go.'
They said, 'The God of the Hebrews has met with us. Please let us go a three-day journey in the wilderness and sacrifice to the LORD our God, lest he strike us with plague or sword.'
The king of Egypt said to them, 'Moses and Aaron, why do you draw the people away from their work? Go to your burdens!'
Pharaoh said, 'Behold, the people of the land are now numerous, and you would have them rest from their burdens.'
That same day Pharaoh commanded the taskmasters of the people and their foremen, saying,
'You shall no longer give straw to the people for making bricks as before; they shall go and gather straw for themselves.'
'But require of them the same quota of bricks they made before; do not reduce it. For they are lazy; that is why they cry, "Let us go and sacrifice to our God."'
'Make the work heavier upon the men so they will be occupied with it and pay no attention to false words.'
The taskmasters of the people and their foremen went out and said to the people, 'Thus says Pharaoh: I will not give you straw.'
'You go and get straw for yourselves wherever you can find it; but nothing of your work will be reduced.'
The people scattered throughout all the land of Egypt to gather stubble for straw.
The taskmasters pressed them, saying, 'Complete your work, each day's matter on its day, just as when straw was supplied.'
The foremen of the sons of Israel, whom Pharaoh's taskmasters had set over them, were beaten. They asked, 'Why have you not completed your quota of bricks yesterday and today as before?'
The foremen of the sons of Israel came and cried out to Pharaoh, saying, 'Why do you treat your servants this way?'
'No straw is given to your servants, yet they tell us, "Make bricks!" Behold, your servants are being beaten, but the fault is with your own people.'
He said, 'You are lazy, you are lazy! That is why you say, "Let us go and sacrifice to the LORD."'
'Now go, work! No straw will be given to you, but you must deliver the quota of bricks.'
The foremen of the sons of Israel saw they were in trouble when they were told, 'You shall not reduce your bricks, each day's matter on its day.'
They met Moses and Aaron, who were standing to meet them, as they came out from Pharaoh.
They said to them, 'May the LORD look upon you and judge, for you have made our scent stink in the eyes of Pharaoh and in the eyes of his servants, putting a sword in their hand to kill us.'
Moses returned to the LORD and said, 'My Lord, why have you done evil to this people? Why is this that you sent me?'
'Since I came to Pharaoh to speak in your name, he has done evil to this people, and you have not delivered your people at all.'
